facility_name: Polaris Forge 1 — Ellendale
pue_source: Applied Digital Completes Phase II Ready for Service at Polaris Forge 1
notes: PUE 1.18, closed-loop waterless DTC cooling, WUE near zero. CoreWeave anchor tenant. 250MW total planned. Equipment vendor opportunity for cooling infrastructure.

facility_name: Stockton1 Floating Data Center
notes: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.15, water-based liquid cooling, 7MW. Floating barge design with patented natural water cooling. Zero water consumption, no chillers. Supports 55kW+/rack. Ideal Solar Steam target.

facility_name: SUPERNAP 7
pue_source: Inside SUPERNAP 8: Switch's Tier IV Data Fortress
notes: PUE 1.18 with proprietary T-SCIF air cooling. No liquid cooling yet — retrofit opportunity as AI tenants demand higher density.

facility_name: Abilene AI Data Center (Stargate)
pue_source: Crusoe announces flagship Abilene data center is live
notes: Massive AI campus with zero-water DTC liquid cooling. Closed-loop system. Phase 1 live, expanding to 1.2GW across 4M sqft. Equipment vendor opportunity at scale.

facility_name: PHX-01 Phoenix
notes: DeltaFlow liquid cooling supporting DTC, RDHx, and immersion up to 300kW/rack. Seamless air-to-liquid transition in live environments. 50+ campuses, 5GW+ planned.

facility_name: Lambda AI Factory — Chicago
pue_source: EdgeConneX and Lambda To Build AI Factory In Chicago With Industry-Leading High-Density Data Center Infrastructure
notes: 23MW AI factory with hybrid liquid-to-chip + air cooling for Lambda. Part of 30MW+ dual-city deal (Chicago + Atlanta). Greenfield.

facility_name: GRC / Endor AI Data Centers
pue_source: GRC Expands Immersion Cooling to the Edge with New ICEraQ Nano System
notes: GRC immersion cooling partnership with Endor Development for giga-scale AI DCs. ICEraQ technology. PUE 1.02-1.05 typical for immersion. First deployments H2 2026. Competitive intelligence.

facility_name: SJC02 Santa Clara
notes: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.1, wall-to-wall liquid, 9MW. 250kW/cabinet.

facility_name: DataBank SLC1 Downtown
notes: 0.5MW small colo. NVIDIA DGX-Ready certified. Liquid cooling ready.

facility_name: Flexential Hillsboro 3
notes: 36MW with built-in liquid cooling. PUE 1.3. High-density compute focus.

facility_name: Sabey SDC Ashburn A
notes: 54MW, 100kW+/rack, liquid-cooled or hybrid. PUE 1.35. Coming online 2026.

facility_name: Compute North TIER 0
notes: PUE 1.04 — lowest verified in dataset. TIER 0 facilities. 41% more efficient than Tier 3. Wind/solar/carbon-free energy mix. Originally crypto, pivoting to AI.

facility_name: Edged Des Moines
notes: PUE 1.15. ThermalWorks waterless cooling. 200kW/rack liquid plug-and-play. 13.2MW. Modular design.

facility_name: Edged Kansas City
notes: PUE 1.15. 26MW. ThermalWorks waterless. 200kW/rack liquid. Nuclear power partnership announced.

facility_name: T5 @ Atlanta I
notes: 7.5MW with liquid cooling. ≤10MW target. Atlanta market. Air + liquid systems.

facility_name: Colovore SJC01 Santa Clara
notes: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.1, wall-to-wall liquid, 4MW. Colovore's original facility. Smaller than SJC02.

facility_name: TACC Frontera (GRC Immersion)
notes: GRC ICEraQ immersion cooling Frontera GPU subsystem. 11+ year relationship. COMPETITOR INTEL — GRC's flagship HPC reference.

facility_name: NREL ESIF HPC Center
notes: WORLD'S LOWEST VERIFIED PUE: 1.036. Warm water DLC captures 97% waste heat. LEED Platinum. Chiller-less design.

facility_name: El Capitan — LLNL
notes: World's fastest supercomputer. PUE 1.08. 100% fanless DLC. 40K AMD MI300A. Saves $12M/yr electricity.

facility_name: Google Oregon (The Dalles)
notes: PUE 1.06-1.07. Google's most efficient US site. ~50% of Google fleet has liquid cooling. 1GW liquid across 2000 TPU pods.

facility_name: Hut 8 Vega — Corpus Christi
notes: 205MW with proprietary in-house DLC. 180kW/rack. Competitive intel — built own cooling.

facility_name: GRC US Air Force (Hill/Tinker AFB)
notes: Government/military GRC immersion. ICEraQ + ElectroSafe fluid. Modular. COMPETITOR INTEL.

facility_name: Cerebras OKC — Inference DC
notes: 10MW dedicated inference. 300+ Cerebras CS-3 Wafer-Scale Engines. Air-cooled (non-GPU arch). Unique compute.

facility_name: Flexential Hillsboro OR — CoreWeave 9MW
notes: 9MW (qualifies!). DLC at >80kW/rack. CoreWeave tenant. Oregon hydro power.

facility_name: xAI Colossus Memphis
notes: WORLD'S LARGEST single-site AI. 2GW. 555K GPUs. Custom DLC for 200K GPUs. Built in 122 days.

facility_name: Microsoft Fairwater Atlanta
notes: World's most powerful AI DC. 4,608 GB300 NVL72. 92.1 exaFLOPS FP4. Custom HXU DLC. Zero water.

facility_name: CoreWeave Lancaster PA
notes: 100MW (→300MW). GB200 NVL72. DLC 130kW/rack. First GB300 commercially deployed.

facility_name: AHEAD Integration Libertyville
notes: 10MW DLC integration facility. Builds liquid-cooled racks for enterprise.

facility_name: Novva Tahoe Reno
notes: 60MW (6x10MW halls). 10MW halls qualify! Near-zero water DLC. 12K gallons total.

facility_name: iM Data Centers Miami
notes: 10MW (qualifies!). Accelsius partnership. Scalable to 50MW. Renewable.

facility_name: T5 Dallas III
notes: 10MW (qualifies!). Air+liquid. GPU-capable. T5 delivered 40K sqft DLC in 12 months.

facility_name: CoreSite CH2 Chicago (STN)
notes: 90% of tenants liquid-cooled! 1,500+ B200 GPUs. 100kW+/cabinet. CoreSite = American Tower.

facility_name: IREN Sweetwater TX
notes: 1.4GW! 700K+ liquid-cooled GPUs capacity. $9.7B Microsoft contract. Energizing April 2026!

facility_name: Vantage Frontier Texas
notes: $25B. 1.4GW. 10 buildings. Liquid cooling for GPUs. Closed-loop.

facility_name: PowerHouse Chesterfield VA
notes: $5B JV. Proprietary DLC (2x air efficiency). CoreWeave anchor. 350 acres Virginia.

facility_name: AWS Rainier Indiana
notes: $11B. 2.2GW. 500K Trainium2. PUE 1.04 (best site). Trainium3 REQUIRES liquid.

facility_name: Meta Hyperion Louisiana
notes: $27B. 2GW. Closed-loop liquid. 1.3M GPUs target. 4M+ sqft. Blue Owl JV.

facility_name: Anthropic FluidStack TX+NY
notes: $50B! Custom liquid-cooled. Claude maker. FluidStack building.

facility_name: Lambda DFW-04 at Aligned
notes: Lambda AI factory at Aligned DFW-04. DeltaFlow DLC. Blackwell GPUs.

facility_name: Lambda LAX01 Vernon CA
notes: 21MW. Blackwell. SoCal. Prime Data Centers.

facility_name: Lambda Kansas City
notes: $500M. 10K+ Blackwell Ultra. Scaling to 100MW+. Microsoft deal.

facility_name: Vultr Springfield OH
notes: $1B. 24K AMD MI355X. Largest AMD GPU deployment. Liquid likely at MI355X density.

facility_name: Sabey Quincy WA
notes: DLC-native campus. Cold plate+sidecars+immersion. PUE ~1.2. Hydropower. Lowest TCO.

facility_name: Flexential Plano TX 13MW
notes: 13MW. DLC >80kW/rack. CoreWeave tenant. Deployed April 2025.

facility_name: HydraVault Chicago
notes: 20MW. Hybrid air+liquid. New entrant. Late 2026.

facility_name: Iron Mountain MIA-1 Miami
notes: 16MW. Liquid cooling. Miami 2026. Major enterprise operator.

facility_name: ServerFarm HTX1 Houston
notes: 500MW+. 250 acres. Water-free liquid. Houston energy corridor.

facility_name: Prime LAX01 Vernon CA
notes: 33MW. Lambda anchor (21MW). Blackwell. Energy Star. Closed-loop.

facility_name: Core Scientific Muskogee OK
notes: 100MW. 70% GPU power. CoreWeave anchor. Crypto→AI pivot.

facility_name: Compass Hoffman Estates Chicago
notes: $10B. 5 DCs. Former Sears HQ. Vertiv CoolPhase Flex.

facility_name: Compass Meridian Mississippi
notes: $10B. 8 buildings. Vertiv hybrid DLC. Also xAI $20B nearby.

facility_name: STACK Loudoun VA
notes: 144MW closed-loop DLC. NoVA = world's largest DC market.

facility_name: EdgeCore US Platform
notes: 1.8GW. DLC baseline. Reno 216MW + Mesa 450MW + VA 300MW.

facility_name: TierPoint TekPark PA
notes: 100MW. Closed-loop DLC. 137-acre campus. $240M financing.

facility_name: Microsoft Phoenix AZ DLC Pilot
notes: First Microsoft zero-water DLC design. Template for all future Azure.

facility_name: Google Michigan 1GW
notes: 1GW campus. 2.7GW clean energy. Google=50%+ fleet liquid-cooled.

facility_name: Nebius Independence MO
notes: Nebius first GW-scale US facility. Beyond EU expansion.

facility_name: Nebius Kansas City AZ (Patmos AI Campus)
notes: Initial 5MW phase operational since Q1 2025 with H200/B200/B300 GPUs, liquid-ready hybrid cooling with 3 cooling towers and district distilled water. Expanding to 40MW by late 2026. Small initial deployment is prime target for cooling vendor engagement before scale-up.

facility_name: TierPoint Spokane SPO03 (Groq LPU)
notes: 3MW facility hosting Groq LPU Inference Engines. LPU is currently air-cooled but TierPoint offers liquid cooling capability for future high-density requirements. Next-gen Groq LPX rack will require liquid cooling. Small facility, strong candidate for cooling upgrade path.

facility_name: Oregon State University CEOAS Data Center (ZutaCore Retrofit)
notes: University research data center retrofitted with ZutaCore HyperCool waterless two-phase direct-to-chip cooling. 30% lower processor temps, 20% higher compute performance. Eliminated 4 air cooling systems. Heat recaptured for campus building heating. Heterogeneous fleet (Dell, HP, Supermicro with NVIDIA/AMD). University HPC centers are typically under 5MW, making this a strong small-facility indicator.

facility_name: Dataprana AI Data Center — Galveston County
notes: Ultra-small 1.5MW AI data center in Galveston County, TX. Joint venture with DC Futureminds. Dataprana specializes in immersion-cooled facilities (flagship 30MW immersion site in La Marque, TX). This is a proof-of-concept site targeting GPUaaS providers. Extremely small facility — ideal for small-scale cooling vendor engagement.

facility_name: UT Arlington ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS Data Center Testbed
notes: DOE-funded data center cooling testbed at UT Arlington, led by Prof. Dereje Agonafer (National Academy of Engineering). Accelsius MR250 250kW two-phase CDU deployed. Target: reduce cooling energy from 40% to under 5% of total IT load. Proof of concept by H1 2026. Partners include JetCool, Intel, Raytheon, HP, NVIDIA, Flexnode. University-scale facility likely under 5MW.

facility_name: Colovore RNO01 — Reno
notes: PUE 1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Colovore's third facility, purpose-built for high-density AI/HPC with up to 250kW per rack via DTC and rear-door cooling. Groundbreaking Feb 2025. Larger than 10MW threshold but PUE and cooling match strongly. Colovore is a liquid-cooling-native operator — competitor landscape intelligence value.

facility_name: Colovore ORD01 — Chicago
notes: PUE ~1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Fourth Colovore location, pre-leasing now for 2027 launch. Larger than 10MW but matches on PUE and cooling. Pre-construction phase means cooling vendor decisions still open.

facility_name: Sabey SDC Austin — TACC Horizon Supercomputer
notes: Sabey's first Texas campus at 85MW total, with 15-20MW allocated to TACC Horizon (largest academic supercomputer in NSF portfolio). Dell-built system using 100% direct liquid cooling with 140kW/cabinet GPU density and immersion cooling. 6x more energy-efficient than air-cooled. 300 petaflops peak FP64. Spring 2026 launch. Campus total is large but TACC deployment is in 15-20MW range.

facility_name: Equinix DC15 CIF — Accelsius NeuCool Deployment
notes: Equinix's Co-Innovation Facility at DC15 Ashburn with Accelsius NeuCool IR80 two-phase DTC system deployed Q3 2025. Partnership catalyzed by shared ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS participation. NeuCool allows 6-8C warmer inlet water = more free cooling. CIF is a showcase/testbed but signals Equinix's cooling vendor evaluation pipeline. Competitive intelligence value for cooling vendors.

facility_name: Colovore Hutto (Project Raptor) — Austin, TX
notes: Colovore's 'Project Raptor' — $500M investment on 30-acre Ironwood Tract near Austin. Part of Colovore's expansion from 10MW to 50MW total. PUE 1.1 (company standard). Liquid cooling + closed-loop water. Construction early 2026. Pre-construction = ideal timing for cooling vendor engagement.

facility_name: Flexnode LIC01 — Long Island City
notes: Ultra-small 1MW edge data center in Long Island City, NYC. ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS-funded ($3.5M) technology: manifold microchannel heatsink + hybrid immersion cooling + additive-manufactured dry cooler. 80kW capable racks with precision immersion and cold plate. GPU and CPU solutions. Digital twin running. Company plans 'a couple more' US sites. Ideal small-facility target.

facility_name: Metrobloks MIA-MB01 — Miami
notes: PUE 1.2 + liquid cooling at 15.2MW. Metrobloks is a 2024-founded developer specializing in high-density liquid-cooled modular colocation in metro areas. Miami facility features up to 150kW/rack, hybrid air + DTC liquid cooling, 22MW reserved utility power. Q4 2026 launch. Ultra-low latency to NAP of the Americas. Pre-construction = cooling vendor decisions open.

facility_name: ORNL Summit Supercomputer — Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility
notes: Summit consumes ~15MW with warm-water (70F) DLC cold plates on all CPUs and GPUs plus rear-door heat exchangers. Cooling drops to 10% of ops cost vs predecessors. Being decommissioned as Frontier/Discovery come online — cooling lessons inform next-gen DOE systems.

facility_name: Argonne Aurora Exascale Supercomputer — ALCF
notes: DOE exascale system consuming ~39MW (60MW facility allocation). Liquid-cooled with 44,000-gallon water system through pipes 4-30 inches diameter. 14 substations provide 60MW for Aurora + building + future systems (Solstice/Equinox). 60,000+ Intel GPUs.

facility_name: TACC Stampede3 Supercomputer — J.J. Pickle Research Campus
notes: NSF flagship supercomputer with Dell PowerEdge XE9640 DLC servers using Intel Data Center GPU Max accelerators. TACC facility total capacity 9MW max (6MW typical). Stampede3 is 10 PFLOPS. DLC enables AI/ML acceleration on the GPU partition. Serving open science 2024-2029.

company: National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC)
facility_name: NERSC Perlmutter Supercomputer — Shyh Wang Hall, LBNL
notes: PUE 1.05-1.08, among world's best. DLC cold plates on 1,536 GPU nodes (NVIDIA A100) and 3,072 CPU nodes. Free cooling via water towers (no mechanical chillers) in Berkeley climate. Up to 250kW per rack. TDP 6.9MW nameplate. Exceptional efficiency reference site.

facility_name: NCSA DeltaAI Supercomputer — National Petascale Computing Facility
notes: 100% water-cooled with zero fans. 4 HPE Cray EX2500 cabinets at 135kW each (~540kW total). NVIDIA H100 and GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. 633 PFLOPS FP16 for AI/ML. Compact 3-rack compute footprint demonstrates extreme DLC density. NSF's most performant GPU resource.

facility_name: LANL Mission & Vision Supercomputers — Los Alamos
notes: Two next-gen supercomputers: Mission (4x faster than Crossroads) and Vision. Direct liquid-cooled HPE Cray GX5000 with NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPUs and Quantum-X800 InfiniBand. Part of DOE $370M investment. Mission supports nuclear stockpile; Vision for AI research. Expected 2027.

facility_name: Sandia HPC Center — Submer Immersion Cooling Pilot
notes: Immersion cooling testbed. Full servers submerged in non-conductive fluid (Submer Technologies, Barcelona). Captures 100% of heat, may reduce power by up to 70%. Zero water evaporation. Waste heat usable for building heating. Case study expected. Complements earlier Astra warm-water cooling (1.2MW, HPE MCS-300 thermosyphon saving 2.5M gal/yr).

facility_name: IREN Childress Horizon 1 — 75MW Liquid-Cooled AI DC
notes: 75MW liquid-cooled AI data center (Horizon 1) at Childress, TX. Direct-to-chip cooling at 200kW/rack for NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. $300-350M investment. Part of 750MW site (450MW energized). Transitioning from Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC. Additional 2GW planned at Sweetwater sites.

facility_name: CyrusOne Aurora Campus CHI1-CHI3 — CME Primary Hub
notes: DOES NOT QUALIFY — no liquid cooling confirmed. 109MW campus, CME Group primary matching engine. Nov 2025 cooling failure caused 10hr trading halt — CyrusOne has since bolstered backup cooling. Air-cooled chillers + closed-loop water. Retrofit opportunity for DLC given Nov 2025 cooling incident. Included for context as major financial sector DC with cooling modernization pressure.

facility_name: CyrusOne San Antonio SAT5-SAT6 — Texas Research Park
notes: 18MW facility at Texas Research Park (5th San Antonio campus). 4 data halls, 279,730 sqft. Intelliscale design supports air cooling upgradeable to DLC/immersion up to 300kW/rack. CyrusOne also operates SAT1 (9MW) and SAT2 (36MW) nearby. Strong retrofit-to-DLC opportunity.

facility_name: Centersquare (Csquare) Nashville — Gateway Drive
notes: DOES NOT CURRENTLY QUALIFY — liquid cooling not yet deployed. 100MW facility in Nashville, part of Centersquare/Csquare $1B expansion. Planning liquid-ready high-density AI aisles. Nashville positioned as AI landing zone. New Gallatin TN campus in development. Strong retrofit opportunity as liquid cooling is deployed.

facility_name: Aligned Data Centers SLC-01 & SLC-02 — West Jordan, UT
notes: SLC-01 (34MW) and SLC-02 (48-60MW) with DeltaFlow liquid cooling for high-density GPU racks up to 300kW. 50+ patented cooling technologies. Secondary loops with valves/taps for DLC. Campus master-planned for 130MW / 780,000 sqft. Acquired by BlackRock consortium for $40B (with NVIDIA as partner).

facility_name: Vantage OH1 — New Albany, Ohio Campus
notes: DOES NOT CURRENTLY QUALIFY — liquid cooling not confirmed for this specific campus. 192MW campus (3x 64MW buildings), $2B+ investment, LEED Silver. Closed-loop chilled water with air-cooled chillers. Vantage broader portfolio includes 250kW+ racks and liquid cooling as baseline for AI campuses. Ohio emerging as major DC market (Intel hub nearby). Strong future DLC opportunity.

facility_name: Compass Meridian Campus — Mississippi (Vertiv CoolPhase Flex)
notes: Each of 8 buildings delivers 40MW IT capacity (320MW total). Compass-Vertiv CoolPhase Flex partnership enables air-to-liquid cooling switchover without downtime. $10B investment. Mississippi Power supplies 500MW. First building expected 2026. Emerging market with strong power availability and incentives.

facility_name: QTS Ashburn Campus — Liquid Cooling Available (DC1-DC5)
notes: QTS Ashburn campus with DC1 (36MW) and expansion through DC5 ($180M). QTS explicitly states 'liquid cooling availability' across standardized data halls. 28-acre campus. DC5 refiled 2025 at 6300 Longhorn Drive. Ashburn is the world's densest data center market.

facility_name: QTS Suwanee 1 — Atlanta Metro (DC1-DC2)
notes: 26MW DC1 at Suwanee with 385,000 sqft and 205,000 sqft raised floor. Campus planned for 100+ MW. QTS also targeting two additional Georgia campuses. Mid-tier facility in growing Atlanta metro market. Cooling type not publicly confirmed — potential DLC retrofit opportunity.

facility_name: QTS Irving Campus — Dallas Metro (DC1-DC5)
notes: DOES NOT CURRENTLY QUALIFY — liquid cooling not confirmed. 75.2MW DC1+DC2 at Irving with DC5 ($180M) under development. Total campus 147MW. Dallas metro is major DC market. Included for context as mid-large QTS facility with potential for DLC retrofit.

facility_name: Microsoft Mount Pleasant — Racine County, WI (Closed-Loop Liquid Cooling)
notes: Massive Microsoft AI DC campus on former Foxconn site. 90%+ closed-loop liquid cooling (filled at construction, continuously recirculated). Phase 1: $3.3B, operational 2026. Phase 2: $4B. 15 additional buildings approved Jan 2026. Wisconsin emerging as major DC market.

facility_name: OpenAI/Oracle/Vantage Lighthouse Campus — Port Washington, WI
notes: 1GW campus (4 data centers) for OpenAI Stargate. Closed-loop liquid cooling confirmed. Part of 4.5GW OpenAI/Oracle capacity. Vantage operating partner. Wisconsin emerging as hyperscale hub alongside Microsoft.

facility_name: NSA Utah Data Center (Intelligence Community Comprehensive National Cybersecurity Initiative)
notes: 60MW power capacity. NSA uses specially designed cooling with water-chilled fluorinert (liquid cooling agent) for classified servers. Feb 2025 Environmental Assessment filed for Utah Data Center campus expansion by USACE. DISA also awarded $931M to HPE for data center modernization (separate from UDC).

facility_name: Cologix COL5 — Columbus, Ohio (AI-Ready Liquid Cooling)
notes: COL5 Phase 1: 25MW, AI-ready with customer choice of air or liquid cooling up to 100kW/rack. Q3 2026 RFS. First colo to complete AI-ready DC in Columbus. Total COL5: 120MW. Licking County 800MW campus ($7B) also planned.

facility_name: Novva Tahoe Reno — 60MW Direct-to-Chip Cooled DC
notes: 60MW across 6x10MW halls with proprietary DTC cooling. Only 12,000 gal water total (closed-loop, zero evaporation). No cooling towers/chillers. 250kW-30MW deployments. CIM Group $2B financing. Novva also has SLC 175MW and LV 75+340MW campuses.

facility_name: TierPoint TekPark — Breinigsville, PA (100MW AI Expansion)
notes: DOES NOT CURRENTLY QUALIFY. 137-acre campus, 100MW expansion H2 2026. Clients use advanced cooling for AI/GPU. $240M acquisition + $1.99B securitization. Strong DLC potential.

facility_name: Meta Beaver Dam AI Campus — Dodge County, WI
notes: Meta's 30th DC worldwide, 26th in US. 700,000 sq ft on 500-acre site. Dry-cooling eliminates water demand. Meta has separately announced company-wide shift to direct-to-chip liquid cooling for AI workloads. Mortenson as GC.

facility_name: Modine Airedale Manufacturing Facility — Franklin, WI
notes: Cooling vendor manufacturing hub. Modine makes Airedale data center cooling solutions. 155,000 sq ft facility, 300+ jobs. Part of $100M multi-year investment. Competitor intelligence — Modine is a cooling vendor, not a customer.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate 'The Barn' — Saline Township, MI
notes: Stargate-branded 1GW+ AI data center campus. Closed-loop non-evaporative liquid cooling (Oracle standard). Three 550K sq ft buildings on 250 acres. Part of OpenAI's $500B Stargate initiative. Walbridge as GC. DTE Energy supplying power with battery storage.

facility_name: xAI MACROHARDRR — Southaven, MS
notes: xAI's $20B expansion into Mississippi. Adjoins Memphis Colossus 1+2 complex. DLC for extreme GPU density. 2GW total cluster capacity. Graywater treatment plant ($80M) to reuse 13M liters/day. Expected Blackwell GB200 and next-gen GPU deployment.

facility_name: Meta Hyperion AI Campus — Richland Parish, LA
notes: World's largest planned AI data center. 5GW total planned, 1.5GW+ Phase 1. $27B joint venture with Blue Owl Capital. 4M sq ft core build. Meta roadmap includes DTC liquid cooling for AI racks. Tent-like prefab construction inspired by xAI. 10 gas-fired power plants ordered.

facility_name: Vultr Springfield AI Supercluster — Clark County, OH (AMD MI355X)
notes: 24,000 AMD MI355X GPUs (1,400W liquid-primary). MI355X spec requires liquid cooling. $901M-$1.3B investment. Former LexisNexis facility. Expected to integrate AMD MI450 and Helios rack-scale infrastructure next.

facility_name: Joule BetterGrid Campus — Millard County, UT (4GW Self-Powered)
notes: Self-powered data center campus on 4,000 acres. Closed-loop DTC liquid cooling saves millions of gallons vs evaporative. Caterpillar G3520K generators + 1.1GWh battery storage. 455MW Q4 2026, scaling to 4GW. Over 4,800 ft elevation for natural cooling efficiency. Combined cooling, heat and power system.

facility_name: Novva Data Centers SLC Campus — West Jordan, UT (DTC AI Expansion)
notes: One of the largest DTC-cooled AI data centers in the world upon completion. Waterless cooling system. Phase 3: 318,000 sq ft, 72MW critical IT. Total campus: 1M sq ft, 175MW when complete. $2B CIM Group financing. Also operates in Colorado Springs and Las Vegas.

facility_name: Novva Colorado Springs Campus — CO (Waterless Cooling Expansion)
notes: 68-acre campus near Denver. 8,000+ hours ambient mountain air cooling. Currently 6MW, expanding to 30-40MW with $200M investment. Plans for 100% renewable energy and waterless cooling. 250,000 sq ft expansion planned.

facility_name: Crusoe Spark Factory — Brighton, CO (Liquid-Cooled Modular AI)
notes: Crusoe manufacturing facility for liquid-cooled modular AI factories (Crusoe Spark). 352,000 sq ft, $200M+. Liquid-cooled version launching H2 2026. First factory-produced modules Q3 2026. This is a manufacturing site for prefab liquid-cooled DC modules.

facility_name: Stargate Project Jupiter — Santa Teresa, NM (Dona Ana County)
notes: $165B Stargate campus. 4 hyperscale buildings on 1,400 acres. Closed-loop DTC liquid cooling — one-time fill of ~10M gallons, then 20K gallons/day average. 700-900MW dedicated microgrid (gas turbines exceeding EPA by 50%). NVIDIA GB200 Blackwell racks. Operational end of 2026.

facility_name: Edged Des Moines — Ankeny, IA (Waterless AI Data Center)
notes: 13MW AI-optimized modular DC. Closed-loop waterless cooling. Supports up to 70kW/rack air and 200kW/rack with plug-and-play liquid cooling integration. Groundbreaking May 2025. Completion 2027.

facility_name: Microsoft West Des Moines 6th Campus (Project Ruthenium) — IA
notes: Microsoft's 6th Iowa campus. Zero-water cooling technology (closed-loop DTC). Part of $5-6B total Iowa investment. Construction start 2026. Also Ginger West campus getting zero-water tech.

facility_name: Meta Kuna Data Center — Kuna, ID
notes: 960,000 sq ft Meta DC. $800M investment. 200MW renewable energy. Closed-loop water-efficient cooling. Water positive by 2030. Operational end of 2026 (delayed from 2025). 1,400 construction workers at peak.

facility_name: ValorC3 Boise — Emerald Street, ID (AI-Ready Colocation)
notes: 10MW AI-ready colocation in Boise. Air and liquid cooling with closed-loop for minimal water waste. Expanding to 12MW before Phase 1 complete. Near Boise City Hall West. Strong small-facility cooling vendor opportunity.

facility_name: Big Sky Digital Infrastructure — Billings, MT (Quantica/EnCap 1GW Campus)
notes: 5,000-acre energy and digital campus outside Billings. 500MW initial, 1GW by 2030. Evaluating multiple cooling approaches including DTC liquid. Renewable + battery energy storage. NorthWestern Energy providing power. EnCap-backed. Cooling decision not yet finalized = opportunity window for vendors.

facility_name: Related Digital Cheyenne Campus — WY (302MW, CoreWeave Anchor)
notes: $1.2B campus. CoreWeave 88MW anchor tenant. Air-cooled chillers, nominal water. 302MW total across 115 acres. Clayco as builder. Phase 1 late 2026. Wyoming's climate supports air cooling efficiency but CoreWeave's GPU workloads may eventually require liquid cooling upgrade.

facility_name: Google Lincoln (Agate) — Lancaster County, NE (2M sq ft)
notes: Google's 3rd Nebraska data center. 2M sq ft on 570+ acres. Could require 1-3GW power (more than all of Lincoln). $1.2B+ investment. Cooling design not yet public — Google has deployed 1GW of liquid cooling globally for TPU pods. Pre-decision opportunity for cooling vendors.

facility_name: Alcove Osawatomie Data Center — Miami County, KS (Predevelopment)
notes: Early-stage predevelopment. 115 acres, 600K sq ft, 50-150MW, $900M-$1.1B. No construction approved, no land sold. 3-year exclusive development agreement. 1M gallons water/day + 150MW power required. Kansas SB 98 tax exemption (20yr) in effect. Very early opportunity window.

facility_name: El Capitan — Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL)
notes: World's fastest supercomputer (#1 TOP500, Nov 2024). 1.742 exaflops. 100% fanless DLC by CoolIT Systems using patented Split-Flow cold plates across 11,000+ compute nodes. 43,808 AMD EPYC Genoa CPUs + 43,808 AMD Instinct MI300A GPUs. ~30 MW peak power. HPE Cray EX platform.

facility_name: Frontier — Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL)
notes: World's first exascale supercomputer (2022). 1.206 exaflops HPL. HPE Cray EX platform, warm-water DLC at 85F. 4x 350HP pumps circulate 6,000 gal/min. AMD EPYC 64C CPUs + AMD Instinct MI250X GPUs. ~29MW IT load, 40MW facility total. 40MW cooling capacity expandable to 70MW.

facility_name: Aurora — Argonne National Laboratory (ANL)
notes: Intel-based exascale supercomputer. 2+ exaflops peak. HPE Cray EX platform with DLC. 63,744 Intel Data Center Max (Ponte Vecchio) GPUs + 21,248 Intel Xeon Max CPUs. 44,000 gal water cooling loop. ~39MW power consumption.

facility_name: Venado — Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL)
notes: LANL's AI supercomputer with 2,560 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips + 920 Grace CPU Superchips. All liquid-cooled HPE Cray EX platform. 10 exaflops peak AI performance. First large-scale NVIDIA Grace CPU deployment in the US. TOP500 #19.

facility_name: Kestrel — National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL)
notes: NREL's flagship HPC. 44 petaflops. PUE 1.03 — among most efficient DCs globally. 100% DLC for CPU nodes. 132 GPU nodes with 4x NVIDIA H100 each + 2,314 CPU nodes. Warm-water heat captured and reused for ESIF building heating and sidewalk de-icing.

facility_name: CyrusOne CHI1 — CME Group Exchange Data Center
notes: CME Group's primary electronic trading platform (CME Globex) runs from this 428,000 sq ft Aurora campus. 92MW at 2N redundancy. Nov 27, 2025 chiller plant failure halted global derivatives trading for ~10 hours — temperatures exceeded 100F. CyrusOne added backup cooling capacity post-incident. High-density financial trading workloads make this a strong candidate for liquid cooling retrofit. CME sold to CyrusOne in 2016 for $130M under 15-year sale-leaseback.

facility_name: Equinix NY5 — Financial Exchange Hub (ZutaCore Liquid Cooling)
notes: Equinix NY5 houses matching engines for world's leading stock exchanges. Running ZutaCore two-phase direct liquid cooling in production on Equinix Metal platform. Part of Equinix's 100+ data center liquid cooling rollout across 45+ metros. Financial trading workloads demand ultra-low-latency, high-density compute. ZutaCore HyperCool uses dielectric fluid that boils at 92F to remove CPU heat.

facility_name: Tesla Cortex / Cortex 2.0 — Giga Texas AI Supercomputer
notes: Tesla's Cortex AI supercomputer at Giga Texas. 50,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs deployed Q4 2024 + 20,000 Tesla-designed AI5 chips planned. Supermicro-provided liquid cooling. 130MW at launch, planned expansion to 500MW by 2026. Cortex 2.0 under construction on north side of facility. Trains Full Self Driving (FSD) and Optimus humanoid robot AI models.

facility_name: Tesla Dojo — Palo Alto (Original)
notes: Tesla's original Dojo supercomputer in Palo Alto. Seven ExaPods planned. Uses custom D1 chips in water-cooled 5x5 Training Tiles. Custom CDU. Power density >200kW per cabinet (MW-class per ExaPod). 2.3MW test tripped local San Jose substation. Direct liquid cooling with bare-die contact cold plates and microchannel dielectric coolant.

facility_name: Shell Skybox Houston — GRC Immersion Cooling HPC
notes: Shell's HPC cluster at Element Critical Skybox Houston. 864 dual-socket AMD EPYC 9654 systems in GRC immersion cooling tanks (100kW/rack). 1,728 CPUs / 165,888 cores. Penguin Solutions hardware. Shell's proprietary immersion cooling fluids. DCD Global Award finalist for North American Data Center Project of the Year. Runs seismic modeling and reservoir simulation workloads.

facility_name: Roche NVIDIA AI Factory — On-Premises Pharma HPC (US + Europe)
notes: Pharmaceutical industry's largest announced GPU footprint. 2,176 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs on-premises across US and Europe. Combined on-prem + cloud exceeds 3,500 GPUs. Announced March 16, 2026 at NVIDIA GTC. Driven via Genentech (South San Francisco). Accelerates drug discovery, diagnostics, manufacturing. Strategic NVIDIA collaboration since 2023. Cooling type not disclosed but Blackwell density strongly implies liquid cooling.

facility_name: Trane Technologies + LiquidStack — Immersion & Direct-to-Chip Platform
notes: M&A MARKET SIGNAL: Trane Technologies acquired LiquidStack (Feb 2026) for end-to-end DC thermal management: chillers + heat rejection + controls + liquid distribution + on-chip cooling. LiquidStack provides high-density liquid, DTC, and immersion cooling for AI/hyperscale. Operations in Carrollton TX + Hong Kong. Now in Trane Americas Commercial HVAC unit. Also partner in Samsung+Chemours Opteon testing (48U tank).

company: US Air Force / GRC (Green Revolution Cooling)
facility_name: GRC ICEtank C3 One — US Air Force Hill AFB (Immersion Cooled)
notes: US Air Force deployed GRC ICEtank immersion-cooled containerized data centers at Hill AFB. C3 One variant uses cooling tower for heat rejection. Tested cumulatively for 3+ years with 100% uptime. PUE of 1.05. 93.1% reduction in cooling PUE. Turnkey system deployable in under 24 hours. GRC working with USAF on next-gen production units. Strong proof-of-concept for military immersion cooling at scale.

company: US Air Force / GRC (Green Revolution Cooling)
facility_name: GRC ICEtank C3 Two — US Air Force Tinker AFB (Immersion Cooled)
notes: US Air Force deployed GRC ICEtank immersion-cooled containerized data centers at Tinker AFB. C3 Two variant uses dry cooler for heat rejection. Tested cumulatively for 3+ years with 100% uptime. PUE of 1.05. Deployable in under 24 hours in wide range of environments. Remote monitoring and control capable.

facility_name: NSA Laboratory for Physical Sciences — GRC Immersion Cooling (ICEraQ)
notes: NSA's Advanced Computing Research team at the Laboratory for Physical Sciences has been a GRC immersion cooling customer since 2012. Achieved PUE of 1.05. 100kW per rack (vs 10-15kW with air). 95% cooling energy cost savings. NSA engineer David Prucnal confirmed substantial CapEx reduction. NSA stated potential to cut future data center construction costs in half. Rapid global deployment capability highlighted.

facility_name: US Naval Research Laboratory — Two-Phase Immersion Cooling Demo (3M/SGI)
notes: NRL hosted ESTCP-funded two-phase immersion cooling demonstration with 3M Novec 649 and SGI IceX hardware. Met DoD energy efficiency and greenhouse gas reduction objectives. Partners: Lawrence Berkeley National Lab, APC/Schneider Electric. Obstacles identified: IT equipment failures in two-phase fluid. Root cause analysis recommended. Important early DoD immersion cooling research site.

facility_name: DISA Defense Enterprise Computing Center (DECC) — Mechanicsburg
notes: DISA DECC Mechanicsburg has end-of-life cooling infrastructure (CRAH units). Replacement contract posted Aug 2024. Separately, DISA awarded $931M DCI modernization OTA to HPE (Nov 2025) for hybrid multi-cloud data center upgrades. Strong retrofit candidate as DISA modernizes aging defense computing infrastructure. One of 6+ active DECC locations.

facility_name: Sandia National Laboratories — Submer Immersion Cooling HPC Test
notes: Sandia National Laboratories formally tested Submer immersion cooling in summer 2024 at its HPC center. Partnership through Adacen Inc. (Albuquerque). Submer provided fluid and equipment; Sandia provided cooling expertise. Estimated 70% power reduction for HPC-AI clusters. Evaluating hardware reliability and infrastructure cost. Case study expected. If positive, could influence DOE-wide adoption across national labs.

company: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) / DOE NNSA
facility_name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory — El Capitan Exascale (Direct Liquid Cooled)
notes: World's fastest supercomputer (Nov 2024). 2.79 ExaFLOPS. 100% direct liquid cooled — zero fans. 11,136 HPE Cray EX nodes with 44,544 AMD MI300A accelerators. ~30MW. Used for nuclear stockpile stewardship. CoolIT Systems provided DLC technology. Top-20 energy efficiency globally (58.89 GF/W). Flagship US government liquid-cooled HPC system.

company: Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) / DOE Office of Science
facility_name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory — Frontier Supercomputer (Direct Liquid Cooled)
notes: America's first exascale supercomputer (2022). HPE Cray EX with direct liquid cooling. 8-30MW range. Four evaporative cooling towers, 9,000-22,700 L/min water flow. TOP500 #2. DOE open science mission. Successor 'Discovery' (next-gen exascale) announced Oct 2025 — also liquid-cooled by HPE. ORNL also getting 'Lux' AI cluster.

company: Argonne National Laboratory / DOE Office of Science
facility_name: Argonne National Laboratory — Aurora Exascale Supercomputer (Liquid Cooled)
notes: Third US exascale supercomputer. ~39MW compute, 60MW infrastructure. Specialized liquid cooling on all blades. Intel Xeon Max + Ponte Vecchio GPUs. 44,000 gallons water through under-floor cooling. New Solstice (100,000 Blackwell GPUs, Oracle/NVIDIA) and Equinox (10,000 Blackwell GPUs) coming H1 2026.

facility_name: US Army / Carlyle — Fort Bliss Hyperscale Data Center (Planned)
notes: US Army conditionally selected Carlyle to build hyperscale data center at Fort Bliss, Texas. 1,384 acres. ~$2B estimated cost. IOC FY2027. Part of Army AI capabilities initiative. Behind-the-meter power and water. Army Corps environmental review pending. Cooling specs not yet disclosed but liquid cooling near-certain for AI-class hyperscale.

facility_name: US Army / CyrusOne — Dugway Proving Ground Hyperscale Data Center (Planned, 1GW)
notes: Massive 1GW data center planned for Dugway Proving Ground. CyrusOne (KKR/BlackRock). 1,201 acres. ~$2B est. IOC FY2029. At 1GW scale, liquid cooling is virtually certain for AI workloads. Utah desert water stress adds urgency for water-efficient cooling (immersion). Behind-the-meter power and water. Army AI initiative. Environmental review pending.

facility_name: TeraWulf Lake Mariner CB-5 -- 160MW Liquid-Cooled AI DC (Fluidstack)
notes: CB-5 is a new 160MW (IT load) purpose-built liquid-cooled data center building at TeraWulf's Lake Mariner campus, the site of a former coal power plant on Lake Ontario. Part of 200+ MW Fluidstack 10-year hosting agreement. Google provided $3.2B financial backstop. Dual 345kV transmission lines. Phase 1 (~40MW) expected H1 2026, full 200+ MW by year-end 2026. Former Bitcoin miner pivoting to AI infrastructure.

facility_name: TeraWulf Abernathy Campus -- 168MW Liquid-Cooled AI DC (Fluidstack)
notes: 168MW IT load (240MW gross) liquid-cooled AI data center at former Century Aluminum smelter site in Abernathy, TX. TeraWulf/Fluidstack JV with project-level financing. Expands TeraWulf's total contracted HPC platform to 510+ MW. Leverages existing transmission capacity. H2 2026 commissioning. Supported by Google financial backstop.

facility_name: Riot Platforms Corsicana -- 600MW AI/HPC Conversion
notes: Riot Platforms' 858-acre Corsicana campus has 1GW ERCOT-approved capacity. 400MW operational for BTC mining with immersion cooling since April 2024. Remaining 600MW formally evaluated for AI/HPC conversion (announced Jan 2025). All further BTC mining buildouts paused. 1.86GW of liquid-cooling-ready power. Riot acquired 593 additional acres in 2025. 77% revenue growth forecast. Converting from pure crypto miner to AI data center operator.

facility_name: Core Scientific Denton TX -- 260MW AI DC (CoreWeave)
notes: 260MW AI-ready campus at Denton, TX. $1.2B expansion announced Feb 2025 (+70MW). 400,000+ sq ft with advanced liquid cooling. CoreWeave deployed 16,000 GPUs. Part of Core Scientific's 590MW total contracted with CoreWeave across 6 sites. Former Bitcoin mining company that emerged from bankruptcy to become AI infrastructure leader. $6.1B total campus investment.

facility_name: Core Scientific Dalton GA -- 175MW AI DC (CoreWeave)
notes: 175MW CoreWeave-contracted AI data center in Dalton, GA. Part of Core Scientific's 590MW total CoreWeave portfolio across 6 sites. Converted from crypto mining to AI GPU infrastructure with direct liquid cooling. Core Scientific operates Dalton 1, 2, 3, and 4 buildings.

facility_name: Core Scientific Marble NC -- 65MW AI DC (CoreWeave)
notes: 65MW CoreWeave-contracted AI data center in Marble, NC (Cherokee County). Former American Thread plant at 155 Palmer Lane. Extensive construction underway converting from crypto mining to NVIDIA GPU clusters. 104MW capacity as of Aug 2024. Direct liquid cooling. Part of Core Scientific's 590MW CoreWeave portfolio.

facility_name: Core Scientific Hunt County TX -- 285MW AI DC (Planned)
notes: 285MW leasable (430MW gross) new AI data center campus on 265 acres in Hunt County, TX, outside Dallas. On-site substation planned. Could begin energizing in 2027, full power by 2029. Core Scientific securing additional greenfield capacity beyond its 6 existing CoreWeave sites.

facility_name: Cipher Mining Barber Lake TX -- 300MW AI DC (AWS)
notes: 300MW AI data center at Barber Lake, TX (near Colorado City). $5.5B 15-year lease with AWS. Both air and liquid cooling. Two-phase delivery: July 2026 (Phase I) and Q4 2026. AWS rent starts Aug 2026. $2B secured note offering (Black Pearl Compute LLC) to fund construction. Originally a 300MW Bitcoin mining site. 587+ acres total.

facility_name: Cipher Mining Colchis TX -- 1GW AI DC (Planned)
notes: 1GW planned data center JV in West Texas named Colchis. 620 acres with fully executed 1GW direct connect agreement with AEP. Cipher holds ~95% equity. Dual interconnection facility to be constructed by AEP. Targeted energization 2028, pending ERCOT review. Adjacent to existing substation.

facility_name: Hut 8 River Bend Campus -- 245MW AI DC (Fluidstack/Anthropic)
notes: 245MW IT capacity, 15-year, $7.0B lease with Fluidstack at River Bend campus in West Feliciana Parish, LA. Anthropic workloads with Google 15-year financial guarantee. Google backstop unlocked billions in JPMorgan/Goldman Sachs project financing. Up to $10B Phase I investment. 592-611 acres. 330MW initial utility capacity, scalable to +1,000MW. Entergy power supply. Former Bitcoin mining company pivoting to AI.

facility_name: Hut 8 Vega -- 205MW DLC Bitcoin/AI Data Center
notes: 205MW Tier I data center with proprietary direct-to-chip liquid cooling. 162,000 sq ft (~5 football fields). 96 custom-designed cooling modules circulating 120,000 gallons of glycol-water solution through closed-loop reverse return system. Supports up to 180kW/rack. Energized June 2025. Currently hosting Bitcoin mining ASICs (~15 EH/s). Hut 8's in-house DLC design could pivot to AI GPU workloads.

facility_name: Bitfarms Moses Lake WA -- 18MW AI/HPC Conversion
notes: First Bitfarms site fully converted to HPC/AI. 6-acre, 18MW campus in Moses Lake, WA. $128M fully funded binding agreement with large publicly traded American multinational (critical infrastructure provider). Liquid cooling for up to 190kW/rack. NVIDIA GB300 GPU validated reference designs. Projected PUE 1.2-1.3. Modular phased construction. Powered by renewable hydroelectric. December 2026 completion. Pursuing both colocation and GPU-as-a-Service.

facility_name: Bitdeer Rockdale TX -- 170MW Crypto/AI DC (Conversion Potential)
notes: 170MW operational at former Alcoa aluminum smelter in Rockdale, TX. 100MW hydro-cooling conversion fully energized for SEALMINER A2 rigs. Bitdeer evaluating conversion of up to 563MW to AI data center. Site is adjacent to Riot Platforms' Whinstone facility. Milam County. Bitdeer expects 200+ MW AI compute by end of 2026.

facility_name: CleanSpark Austin County TX -- 285MW AI DC (Submer Immersion)
notes: 285MW AI data center campus on 271 acres in Austin County, TX. Partnership with Submer for liquid immersion cooling. Engineered as next-gen AI factory for high-density GPU clusters. Substation construction underway, long-lead equipment secured. Fast-track schedule for H1 2027 energization. $9-11M/MW build cost. CleanSpark pivoting from 1.3GW BTC mining portfolio to AI. Part of 2GW+ pipeline with Submer.

facility_name: CleanSpark Brazoria County TX -- 600MW AI DC (Planned)
notes: 600MW potential AI data center on 447 acres in Brazoria County, TX (~40 miles south of Houston). 300MW initial demand load with expansion to 600MW. Transmission-level power with long-term facilities extension agreement. Second CleanSpark Texas AI site after Austin County (285MW). Combined Texas potential: 885MW+. Land deal expected to close Q1 2026.

facility_name: Applied Digital Polaris Forge 1 -- 400MW AI Factory (CoreWeave)
notes: 400MW AI Factory in Ellendale, ND. Building 1 (100MW) operational since Nov 2025. Proprietary closed-loop waterless DTC cooling design. Near-zero water consumption. 220+ days/year free cooling. CoreWeave is tenant for 400MW across 3 lease agreements. $2.7B lifecycle cost savings over 30 years vs comparable sites. Scalable to 1GW+. Macquarie $787.5M financing. Applied Digital transitioned from crypto mining to AI infrastructure.

facility_name: Applied Digital Polaris Forge 2 -- 280MW AI Factory (Harwood ND)
notes: 280MW AI Factory near Harwood, ND. $3B investment. $5B lease with unnamed U.S. investment-grade hyperscaler. 900+ acres, Cass County Electric Cooperative power. Projected PUE 1.18, near-zero water consumption. Closed-loop liquid cooling. Initial 200MW across two buildings in 2026, full 280MW early 2027. Applied Digital's second major AI campus in North Dakota.

facility_name: Marathon Digital (MARA) Granbury TX -- 300MW Immersion-Cooled DC
notes: 300MW crypto mining data center with two-phase immersion cooling at Granbury, TX (Hood County). MARA developed 2PIC700 immersion system (2-4x power density, 60% cooling cost reduction) but later discontinued for AI in favor of DTC. AI inference deployed at site in modular air-cooled containers. MARA targets 50% revenue from international markets by 2028. 1.5GW total capacity target. Adjacent to wind farm.

facility_name: Cipher Mining Black Pearl TX -- 300MW AI/Crypto DC (AWS)
notes: 300MW data center at Black Pearl site in Wink, TX (Winkler County). Phase 1 (150MW) operational. Originally 250MW air-cooled + 50MW liquid-cooled crypto mining. $2B secured note offering (Black Pearl Compute LLC, due 2031) to fund AI conversion. Part of $5.5B AWS lease. Cipher also holds 337 additional acres of adjacent land.

facility_name: Soluna Project Dorothy 3 -- AI DC (Briscoe Wind Farm)
notes: 150MW wind-powered AI data center campus at Briscoe County, TX. $53M wind farm acquisition. Project Dorothy 3 extends Dorothy 1/2 campus. Soluna provides AI compute to Atlas Cloud. Proprietary cooling with radiators on chips (60+ kW/rack, water-free). 4.3GW pipeline. Launched AI infrastructure initiative. Dorothy 2 (48MW) fully operational Nov 2025. Project Kati 1 (83MW) energizing H1 2026.

facility_name: Core Scientific Austin TX -- 16MW AI DC (CoreWeave)
notes: 16MW CoreWeave-leased AI data center at 3301 Hibbetts Rd, Austin, TX. 8-year lease, 118,000 sq ft. $100M+ total lease value. Formerly HP hosting/HPC facility. Part of Core Scientific's 590MW CoreWeave portfolio. Converted from crypto mining.

facility_name: Horizon Supercomputer -- TACC (NSF LCCF)
notes: Largest academic supercomputer in the world (when complete). 300 PFLOP/s. 1 million CPU cores + 4,000 NVIDIA GPUs. 15-20MW new data center. Dell IRSS DLC servers. 140kW per GPU cabinet. Propylene glycol direct-to-chip + chilled water rear-door radiators. 400,000 gallons/hour water flow. 6x more energy efficient than predecessors. NSF LCCF flagship.

company: National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) / CISL
facility_name: Derecho -- NCAR-Wyoming Supercomputing Center
notes: 19.87 PFLOP/s climate research supercomputer. $35M system. Liquid-cooled with evaporative cooling towers providing free cooling 96% of year using Cheyenne's cool climate. Waste heat heats admin areas and melts snow/ice on walkways. NCAR-Wyoming facility currently under NSF transition planning. Critical for US Earth system science.

facility_name: MIT Lincoln Laboratory Supercomputing Center (LLSC)
notes: World's largest interactive on-demand supercomputing center. Houses TX-GAIA (4.7 PFLOP/s). Hosted at MGHPCC (LEED Platinum, first academic DC with that status). 15MW utility connection, 70% hydroelectric. 10MW compute capacity. Birthplace of JetCool microconvective DLC technology (Project JETS, spun out 2019, acquired by Flex 2024). Also houses Harvard Cannon cluster.

facility_name: LillyPod AI Factory -- Eli Lilly Indianapolis HQ
notes: Pharma industry's largest wholly-owned AI supercomputer. NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with 1,016 Blackwell Ultra GPUs delivering 9,000+ petaflops AI performance. 30,000 sq ft facility at Indianapolis HQ. Uses existing chilled water infrastructure for liquid cooling. 100% renewable energy. Applications: protein diffusion models, small-molecule GNN, genomics foundation models, digital twins, precision medicine imaging. Separate $1B co-innovation lab with NVIDIA in San Francisco planned with Vera Rubin chips.

facility_name: BioHive-2 Supercomputer -- Recursion Pharmaceuticals
notes: First pharma company to own TOP500-ranked supercomputer (#35 as of May 2024). NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with 63 DGX H100 systems totaling 504 H100 GPUs, Quantum-2 InfiniBand networking. 2 exaflops AI performance. 4x faster than predecessor BioHive-1. Powers AI drug discovery across Recursion + Valence Labs teams. Also acquired Exscientia for ~$688M in 2024. NVIDIA invested $50M directly.

facility_name: Mayo Clinic DGX SuperPOD -- Rochester
notes: First major health system to deploy NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with DGX B200 Blackwell systems. 128 GPUs. Powers digital pathology foundation model (Atlas, trained on 1.2M+ whole-slide images in partnership with Aignostics), precision medicine, drug discovery, clinical AI. Shortens training from months to weeks. 20M+ digitized pathology slides dataset. Cancer AI Alliance member. NVIDIA Blackwell B200 SuperPOD specification typically requires liquid cooling.

facility_name: NIH Biowulf HPC Cluster -- Bethesda Data Center
notes: Most powerful HPC system in the US dedicated to biomedical research. 105,000+ processors, 4,200+ nodes (97,000 cores), 25+ PB primary storage. FY2024: 1 billion+ core-hours, ~6 million GPU-hours. Under-floor chilled water cooling with recently upgraded pumps. Used for genomics, cancer research, drug discovery, structural biology, neuroimaging. Power and cooling are primary constraints on further expansion. Supports ~500 scientific applications.

facility_name: St. Jude Children's Research Hospital Data Center -- Memphis
notes: Largest collection of pediatric cancer genomics data ever assembled. HPC cluster: 300+ general nodes (25,000 cores), ~60 GPU nodes (400+ GPUs), 5.3 PFLOPS compute, 150+ PB research storage (20 PB secure/compliant). St. Jude Cloud shares deidentified pediatric genomics data. Center of Excellence for Data-Driven Discovery (D3). Whole genome/exome sequencing, AI-driven cancer research. Growing GPU estate for AI workloads creates liquid cooling opportunity.

facility_name: Memorial Sloan Kettering IRIS Supercluster / Cluster of Discovery
notes: World-leading cancer center with dedicated AI supercluster infrastructure. IRIS superclusters (Lux for genomics processing, Phoenix for clinical sequencing). Cluster of Discovery is newest supercomputer, reducing wall clock time by 30x. WEKA Data Platform for high-performance storage. New computational campus spans Computational Oncology, Radiology Informatics, Clinical Bioinformatics, and HPC. AWS collaboration (announced Feb 2025) for AI-driven cancer innovation using deidentified genomic, imaging, and clinical data.

facility_name: Genentech / Roche gRED -- South San Francisco (NVIDIA DGX Cloud)
notes: Roche's US-based drug discovery arm with dedicated NVIDIA DGX Cloud access for AI supercomputing. Multi-year strategic research collaboration with NVIDIA (announced Nov 2023). 'Lab in the Loop' strategy: experimental data feeds ML models, predictions tested in wet lab, iterative refinement. NVIDIA BioNeMo for generative AI drug discovery. Part of Roche's broader 3,500+ GPU hybrid-cloud AI factory. One of the world's largest biomedical datasets.

facility_name: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center -- Cancer AI Alliance (Seattle)
notes: Coordinator of Cancer AI Alliance, a multi-institution initiative with $40M+ funding from AWS, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Deloitte, Slalom. Building GPU-equipped infrastructure for processing health records, pathology, genomics, medical images at scale. In-house data science infrastructure (Translational Data Science IRC, 2018) with AWS hybrid cloud. Machine learning, deep neural networks, NLP for genetic disease discovery and personalized treatments. AWS committed $10M directly.

facility_name: Shell HPC Data Center -- Skybox Houston One, Katy TX (1.2MW Immersion-Cooled)
notes: Shell deployed 864 dual-socket servers (AMD EPYC 9654, 96-core, 4th Gen) in 6 GRC ICEraQ Series 10 Duo immersion cooling pods at Skybox Houston One in Katy, Texas. Total: 1,728 processors, 165,888 cores, 1.2MW compute. Servers fully submerged in Shell's own ElectroSafe immersion cooling fluid. Each GRC pod cools 200kW using warm facility water (up to 100kW per 42U rack with 90F water). Net-zero operational emissions claimed via Shell Energy North America 100% renewable power. Penguin Solutions provided the HPC cluster.

facility_name: DUG Technology HPC DC -- Skybox Houston, TX (15MW Immersion-Cooled, Oil & Gas)
notes: DUG Technology, Australia's leading geoscience HPC provider, leased 15MW at Skybox Datacenters in Houston's Energy Corridor. 40,000 servers fully immersed in DUG Cool dielectric fluid across 720+ enclosures, delivering 250 petaflops. DUG Cool is a patented immersion system refined over a decade, reducing power consumption by up to 51% vs air cooling by eliminating refrigerated cooling and fans. Serves global oil & gas industry for seismic data processing. Also the largest commercial user of Intel Xeon Phi co-processors (historically). Previously used GRC CarnotJet System at Perth facility.

facility_name: Subsea Cloud Jules Verne Pod -- Port Angeles
notes: Pressure-equalized underwater data center pod submerged ~9m near Port Angeles, WA. 20-foot container-sized pod with 16 server racks (~800 servers) delivering ~1MW IT capacity. Connected to shore via 100 Gbps fiber. Unique pressure-equalized design eliminates structural stress. Servers in dielectric immersion fluid. Claimed PUE of 0.88-1.01 (effectively no cooling overhead). 40% CO2 reduction vs traditional DCs. Two additional deployments planned: Njord01 (Gulf of Mexico) and Manannan (North Sea, 200-300m depth).

facility_name: NetworkOcean Underwater Data Center -- San Francisco Bay (Proposed)
notes: Y Combinator-backed startup (Summer 2024 batch) attempting to build underwater GPU data centers. Claims 2,048 Nvidia H100 GPUs available at $2.10/GPU/hr. 0.5MW capsule under development for San Francisco Bay test. Ran into major regulatory issues -- BCDC and SF Regional Water Quality Control Board warned of fines for unpermitted deployment. Now experimenting with floating server approach. ~$1M raised. High ambition, early stage, regulatory risk.

facility_name: DeepGreen Western Passage Tidal-Powered Underwater Data Center (Proposed)
notes: Proposed 51MW tidal energy + subsea AI data center in Western Passage, Maine. 170 tidal turbines + 34 AI compute pods on ocean floor using 'universal docking cradles.' $415M estimated cost. FERC preliminary permit filed Feb 2026 for 48-month environmental study. Would also supply power to Eastport residents and the Passamaquoddy Tribe. Active infrastructure occupies ~27 acres of a 400-acre permit area. Developer also exploring Cook Inlet, Alaska. Very early stage -- years from operational.

facility_name: Switch Citadel Campus -- EVO AI Factories (Desert, Hybrid Liquid Cooling)
notes: Massive 7.2 million sqft desert campus in Tahoe Reno Industrial Center, Nevada with 650MW capacity. Switch's proprietary EVO design supports up to 2MW per rack via 5 separate fluidic cooling pathways at different pressures and temperatures. Hybrid air + direct-to-chip liquid cooling aligned with Nvidia DGX and MGX roadmaps. 20-foot reinforced concrete walls provide extreme physical security. Reclaimed water for cooling towers. Switch raised $20B since 2024 through sustainable financing. AI factory focus.

facility_name: Iron Mountain WPA-1 -- The Underground (Former Limestone Mine, Geothermal-Cooled)
notes: Iron Mountain's iconic 'Underground' data center sits 200 feet below ground in a former limestone mine in Boyers, PA. 330,000 sqft facility on 315-acre campus. 15.5MW capacity. Geothermal cooling uses a 35-acre underground lake for chilled water -- natural 52F ambient temperature year-round eliminates traditional chillers. Physical security from 200ft of limestone above. Serves enterprise, government, and financial services clients. Part of DOE Better Buildings program for geothermal cooling system.

facility_name: Bluebird Underground Data Center (Former Limestone Mine)
notes: Data center 85 feet below ground in a former Burlington Limestone mine in Springfield, Missouri. 2-square-mile block of honeycombed limestone with 5M+ sqft excavated. 80,000 sqft total facility with 29,000 sqft whitespace. 6MW off-grid power with 3 diverse utility feeds. Natural 64-68F year-round temperature. Closed-loop chilled water cooling. Protected from earthquakes, floods, extreme weather. $11.5M expansion completed 2020.

facility_name: InfoBunker -- Military-Grade Underground Data Center (Iowa)
notes: Military-grade hardened data center 50+ feet underground. 3-foot thick reinforced concrete. Designed to survive a 20-megaton nuclear blast from 2.5 miles. 10MW utility power via dedicated substation (ring-fed from 2 other substations). 3x 750kVA turbine diesel backup generators (N+2 or 2N+1). EMP shielded. 10-tonne blast doors. Dry cooling and economizer mode for 6-8 months/year. 65,000 sqft total (38,000 sqft DC). SSAE 16, SOC 1/2/3, PCI-DSS, HIPAA compliant.
